mnemonics: SIP (Single Import Path): Think of a single straw (import) sipping from a specific juice box (package). Use the full path for a single class, not the whole fridge.
keyPoints: The `import` keyword allows you to use classes from other packages without their fully qualified names.
You can import a single class using `import package.name.ClassName;` or all classes from a package using `import package.name.*;`.
The `java.lang` package (containing System, String, etc.) is automatically imported and does not require an explicit import statement.
commonMistakes: Using a wildcard import (e.g., `import java.util.*;`) when you only need one or two specific classes from the package, which can lead to ambiguity and reduced code clarity.
Trying to import classes from the default package (a package with no name) which is not allowed; all classes must belong to a named package to be imported.
